title: lavatera cretica var. sylvestris
intro: Lavatera cretica var. sylvestris, also known as Rock Rose or Sea Mallow, is a variety of the Lavatera cretica plant, a type of flowering plant in the mallow family (Malvaceae). The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory effects, which can be attributed to the presence of flavonoids and glycosides. The health benefits of Rock Rose include its ability to soothe skin irritations, reduce inflammation, and act as a natural antiseptic, making it a popular remedy for wound healing and skin conditions such as eczema and acne. The herbal preparations of Rock Rose include infusions, tinctures, and salves, which can be used topically or internally to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
